title: The Seven Essential Factors for AI Ethics & Policy
description: A flowchart illustrating the seven interconnected factors that must be considered when developing ethical AI public policy, showing how they influence each other to create responsible governance.
relationships: Accountability ensures that mechanisms are in place to audit and redress AI systems, which is foundational to building Public Trust. Transparency, or 'Explainability', allows for this accountability and is necessary for assessing Fairness. Fairness & Non-Discrimination is a core goal that depends on transparent processes and safe, reliable systems. Privacy protections are a key Human-Centered Value and are a prerequisite for trust. Safety & Reliability is the technical foundation that ensures AI systems do not cause harm and operate as intended. Human-Centered Values ensure that AI benefits society and operates in accordance with human rights, which is the ultimate goal of Governance. Finally, effective Governance integrates all factors to foster and maintain Public Trust, creating a feedback loop that strengthens the entire system.
